has established a separate public agency to screen projects 
which raise the environmental impact issue. 
If it is determined 
by this screening body that such impa.ct may exist, the applicant 
then retains a private consultant for the purpose of preparing 
an environmental impact report for presentation to the Planning 
Commission. 
If the screening body does not find the potential 
for an adverse impact, the project is forwarded directly to 
the Planning Commission for consideration on other issues. 
The screening body is not composed of nor controlled by 
members of the Planning Commission. 
In preparing an environmental impact report, the 
private consultant is working pursuant to a contractual 
agreement with the applicant. 
While environmenta1 impact 
reports are supposed to contain independent and objective 
analysis, the fact that the permit applicant selects and 
pays the preparer of the impact report creates a situation 
where the preparer may slant his environmental analysis to 
assure future business from the applicant. 
Neither the 
Planning Commission nor any other agency of the City prepares 
any independent analysis of the environmental impact of a 
project. 
Accordingly, the Planning Commiss on frequently 
relies heavily on the facts supplied and conclusions reached 
by the private consultant concerni 
the matter. 
The Planning 
Commission considers the environmental issue as a threshold 
matter and if it concludes that an adverse impact will not 
result from appr.oval of the pr.oject, it will then proceed to 
consider the other issues concerning 
project. 
In the instant case, t 
spouse of one of the 
Planning Commissioners is a salaried f 
I-time employee of 
an environmental consulting firm which 
epares reports of 
the type di~7ussed above for consideration by the Planni 
Commission.-
The spouse evaluates the biological 
ct of 
a project and his conclusions are a part of the total env ron-
mental impact report. 
The question thus presented is: 
n light of the 
provisions of Government Code Section 87100, when must the 
Planning Commissioner in question refrain f om voting, or

The spouse does not, however, have an ownership 
interest in the firm.

We also think that in most circumstances this 
ef 
t w 11 be "material" within the meaning of Government 
Code Section 87103. 
The Commission has issued regulations 
defining "material financial effect," 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
1870 (a) I which provides in relevant part: 
The financial effect of a governmental decision on 
a financial interest of a public official is material 
if, at the time the official makes, participates 
in making or attempts to use his or her-official 
position to influence the making of the decision, 
in light of all the circumstances and facts known 
at the time of the decision, the official knows or 
has reason to know that the existence of the financial 
interest might interfere with the official's performance 
of his or her duties in an impartial manner free 
from bias. 
In light of all the circumstances surrounding the 
instant case, we think a reasonable person would conclude 
that the Planning Commissioner might not be impartial in 
evaluating reports submitted by her husband or her husband's 
firm. 
In a case such as this, the Planning Commissioner may 
be biased in her judgment concerning a project's environme tal 
impact because of the financial interest of her husband and 
her husband's firm in seeing the project approved on environ-
mental grounds. 
Lastly, the decision clear y will affect 
the firm in question in a manner different from its effect 
on the public generally. 
Accordingly, we believe that Government Code Sections 
87100 and 87103 require the Planning Commission to refrain 
from making or participating in any decisions of the Commission 
concerning the existence of adverse environmental impact of 
projects where the consulting firm in question has prepared 
the environmental impact report. 
The Commissioner is not 
disqualified from participating in any deci ions concerning 
those projects which raise issues unrelated to the threshold 
environmental issue.

sections.question: In light of the provisions of Government Code Section 87100, when must the Planning Commissioner in question refrain from voting, or otherwise participating in the consideration of certain matters coming before the Commission?
sections.conclusion: Accordingly, we believe that Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 require the Planning Commission to refrain from making or participating in any decisions of the Commission concerning the existence of adverse environmental impact of projects where the consulting firm in question has prepared the environmental impact report. The Commissioner is not disqualified from participating in any decisions concerning those projects which raise issues unrelated to the threshold environmental issue.

sections.question_synthetic: When must a Planning Commissioner recuse herself from voting on environmental impact reports prepared by an environmental consulting firm where the Commissioner's spouse is a salaried employee of that firm, given Government Code Section 87100 conflict of interest provisions?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Planning Commissioner must recuse herself from decisions concerning the environmental impact analysis of projects where her spouse's consulting firm prepared the environmental impact report, as the financial interest of her spouse in project approval would create a material financial effect and potential bias under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103. However, she may participate in decisions on other non-environmental issues related to those projects.
sections.parsing_notes: Mixed formats: modern, ocr; LLM: Document has OCR degradation but question and conclusion sections are clearly identifiable. Document declined to issue formal opinion but provided advice under Government Code Section 3114(b). Some text fragmentation in OCR version did not prevent accurate extraction of key sections.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In light of the provisions of Government Code Section 87100, when must the Planning Commissioner in question refrain from voting, or otherwise participating in the consideration of certain matters coming before the Commission?

CONCLUSION: Accordingly, we believe that Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103 require the Planning Commission to refrain from making or participating in any decisions of the Commission concerning the existence of adverse environmental impact of projects where the consulting firm in question has prepared the environmental impact report. The Commissioner is not disqualified from participating in any decisions concerning those projects which raise issues unrelated to the threshold environmental issue.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the City of Oceanside that a Planning Commissioner whose spouse is a salaried employee of an environmental consulting firm that prepares impact reports for projects before the Commission must recuse herself from decisions on the environmental impact portion of those projects due to conflicts of interest under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103.
